items.title: USDA finalizes tougher SNAP retailer stocking standards; compliance begins Nov. 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s final rule raises SNAP retailer staple-food requirements to 7 varieties across 4 staple categories (protein, grains, dairy, and fruits/vegetables), more than doubling the previous standard. USDA says SNAP-authorized retailers process over $90 billion per year (about $236 million per day) in benefits, and compliance with the new stocking standards starts Nov. 4, 2026.
items.source: USDA Food and Nutrition Service / USDA

items.title: CMS issues Medicaid community engagement (work) rule: 80 hours/month; states must implement by Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: CMS released an interim final rule implementing a new Medicaid community engagement requirement for certain non-pregnant adults ages 19–64 in the Medicaid adult group. The rule sets a standard of 80 hours per month of qualifying activities (or an income-equivalent threshold of $580/month in 2026, based on 80 hours at the federal minimum wage) and says states must generally implement the requirement by Jan. 1, 2027.

items.title: WIC FY 2026 fruit-and-vegetable benefit amounts: $26 children, $48 pregnant/postpartum, $52 breastfeeding
items.summary: USDA set WIC’s FY 2026 cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/CVB) amounts at $26 per month for children, $48 per month for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 per month for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo defines FY 2026 as Oct. 1, 2025 through Sept. 30, 2026.
items.source: USDA Food and Nutrition Service (WIC Policy Memorandum)

items.title: USDA posts WIC income eligibility guidelines for 2026–27 (effective July 1, 2026–June 30, 2027)
items.summary: USDA published WIC income eligibility guidelines effective July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. Examples on the federal guideline table include $15,960 annual income for a 1-person household (100% of poverty) and $33,000 for a 4-person household (100% of poverty).

items.title: Federal Register sets 2026–27 FAFSA and Title IV processing deadlines: June 30, 2027 FAFSA receipt; Sept. 11, 2027 corrections
items.summary: A June 22, 2026 Federal Register notice sets the deadline for receipt of a 2026–27 FAFSA by the Department’s FAFSA Processing System (FPS) as June 30, 2027. The notice also sets Sept. 11, 2027 as the deadline for corrections (and related changes/duplicate requests), and says schools must receive an ISIR or FAFSA Submission Summary no later than the student’s last date of enrollment for 2026–27 or Sept. 18, 2027 (whichever is earlier).

items.title: USDA confirms SNAP retailer stocking-rule compliance date: Nov. 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP retailer guidance page says the final rule “Updated Staple Food Stocking Standards for Retailers in SNAP” requires retailers to comply starting Nov. 4, 2026. The agency says it will publish additional retailer guidance and update training materials ahead of that date.

items.title: Medicaid work-rule rollout: CMS outlines verification steps and a 30-day response window if compliance can’t be verified
items.summary: CMS says states must verify compliance with the Medicaid community engagement requirement at application and renewal, and (optionally) at more frequent intervals. If a state can’t verify compliance, it must send a noncompliance notice and give the person 30 calendar days to show they met the requirement or qualify for an exemption.
